proposals.title: UP_TRI × Bear: n=97, 93.7% WR, Tier M → boost candidate (TAKE_SMALL)

claim.what: Promote boost_pattern: signal=UP_TRI, regime=Bear (Tier B / TAKE_SMALL)
claim.expected_effect: Surface as Tier-B boost on cohort matches; observed +42.2pp edge over per-signal baseline

evidence: Wilson 95% lower bound 0.8690 well above Tier M floor — sample size adequate
R-multiple 0.61 below Tier S threshold (1.0); promotion is to Tier M (B/TAKE_SMALL) only, not Tier S (A/TAKE_FULL)
risks: Cohort tier may drift on next archive comparison; demotion auto-fires per §3
R-multiple distribution under D-8 review (Day-6 forced-exit dominance per project_anchor §7 D-8)

Portfolio is heavily concentrated in UP_TRI (30/31) and Bull regime (0.871 above 0.80 threshold); if regime flips to Bear, dormant Bear-gated UP_TRI promotion would immediately fire on top of existing UP_TRI concentration, amplifying single-signal risk
approval would propagate overlap to mini_scanner_rules.json

proposed_rule_entry.reason: brain_step4: cohort Tier M, n=97, wr=93.7%, edge +42.2pp

decision_metadata.llm_enrichment_rationale: Step 4 tier classification remains robust (n=97, Wilson lower 0.869, +42.2pp edge over UP_TRI baseline) with promotion correctly capped at Tier M/TAKE_SMALL given R-multiple 0.61 below the Tier S threshold. Cohort is regime-gated to Bear while current regime is Choppy (shifting, day 4), so the cohort is dormant and the flagged Bull concentration (0.871) is not compounded by a Bear-gated promotion. Consistent with continuous prior approvals (2026-04-29 through 2026-07-16) and no trader rejection on record.

proposals.title: BULL_PROXY × Bear: n=17, 88.2% WR, Tier M → boost candidate (TAKE_SMALL)

claim.what: Promote boost_pattern: signal=BULL_PROXY, regime=Bear (Tier B / TAKE_SMALL)
claim.expected_effect: Surface as Tier-B boost on cohort matches; observed +33.3pp edge over per-signal baseline

evidence: Wilson 95% lower bound 0.6566 well above Tier M floor — sample size adequate
R-multiple 0.98 below Tier S threshold (1.0); promotion is to Tier M (B/TAKE_SMALL) only, not Tier S (A/TAKE_FULL)
risks: Cohort tier may drift on next archive comparison; demotion auto-fires per §3
R-multiple distribution under D-8 review (Day-6 forced-exit dominance per project_anchor §7 D-8)

Portfolio is heavily Bull-concentrated (87.1%) and UP_TRI-dominant (30/31); if regime flips to Bear abruptly, BULL_PROXY Bear-gated boosts will activate against a portfolio built for the opposite regime — monitor transition risk
approval would propagate redundant to mini_scanner_rules.json

proposed_rule_entry.reason: brain_step4: cohort Tier M, n=17, wr=88.2%, edge +33.3pp

decision_metadata.llm_enrichment_rationale: Step 4 tier classification remains robust (n=17, Wilson lower 0.6566 above Tier M floor, +33.3pp edge over BULL_PROXY baseline of 54.9%) with promotion correctly capped at Tier M/TAKE_SMALL given R-multiple 0.98 (<1.0). Cohort is regime-gated to Bear while current regime is Choppy (shifting, day 4), so the cohort is dormant; portfolio Bear exposure is 0 and the flagged 87.1% Bull concentration is on a different regime, so a Bear-gated BULL_PROXY promotion does not compound concentration risk and may diversify when Bear re-emerges. Consistent with continuous prior approvals of this exact cohort (2026-06-19 through 07-16) with no trader rejection on record.

proposals.title: Exposure concentration: by_regime + by_signal_type = Bull regime (87.1%) + UP_TRI signal (96.8%) (87.1%)

claim.what: Concentration risk: by_regime + by_signal_type = Bull regime (87.1%) + UP_TRI signal (96.8%); recommended action: Pause new UP_TRI entries in Bull regime until regime share drops below 0.80 via natural Day-6 exits OR until Bull×UP_TRI cohort promotes beyond Candidate tier; prioritize any BULL_PROXY or non-Bull-regime opportunities to diversify
claim.expected_effect: Trader awareness of tail risk: A Bull→Choppy or Bull→Bear regime flip would simultaneously invalidate ~87% of open positions with no cohort-validated edge to justify holding through the transition; all 30 UP_TRI longs would face correlated adverse move on the same directional thesis

evidence: Matched Bull×UP_TRI cohort at Candidate tier only (n=60) — unvalidated edge
risks: Recommendation may be over-cautious; concentration may be edge-validated
Trader may already be aware; informational only

proposals.reversibility: Informational warning; no system mutation. Suppresses on next run if concentration drops below threshold.

decision_metadata.recommended_action: Pause new UP_TRI entries in Bull regime until regime share drops below 0.80 via natural Day-6 exits OR until Bull×UP_TRI cohort promotes beyond Candidate tier; prioritize any BULL_PROXY or non-Bull-regime opportunities to diversify
decision_metadata.tail_risk_scenario: A Bull→Choppy or Bull→Bear regime flip would simultaneously invalidate ~87% of open positions with no cohort-validated edge to justify holding through the transition; all 30 UP_TRI longs would face correlated adverse move on the same directional thesis
decision_metadata.llm_rationale: Regime concentration is triggered at 87.1% Bull (above the 0.80 threshold) AND UP_TRI dominates signal mix at 30/31 = 96.8% (above 0.85 threshold), making the book a near-monoculture directional long in Bull regime. The matched Bull×UP_TRI cohort sits only at Candidate tier (n=60) — unvalidated edge — so the dual concentration is unvalidated exposure, not validated edge.
